item.title: Avionics Technician- 14x14 - Houma, LA
Aviation Maintenance Technician - 14x14 - Houma, LA
item.description:
$32.00–$46.00 USD DOE
The A&P Field‑Based Helicopter Aviation Maintenance Technician is responsible for ensuring Bristow aircraft are maintained in a safe, airworthy, and mission‑ready condition in full compliance with all applicable regulatory requirements, airworthiness directives, and Bristow Group maintenance policies and procedures.
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: - Maintain close coordination with the Lead Aviation Maintenance Technician (AMT) to provide continuous, accurate status of assigned rotor‑wing aircraft, including current discrepancies, upcoming 135‑scheduled maintenance, and anticipated downtime, in alignment with Bristow procedures and operational requirements. - Perform inspection, maintenance, modification, and repair of Bristow rotor‑wing aircraft—including airframe, dynamic components, engines, and associated systems—in accordance with Bristow’s approved manuals, manufacturer documentation, and all applicable Federal Air Regulations (Part 135). - Plan, coordinate, and schedule maintenance activities for GoA 135 aircraft in collaboration with the Lead AMT, considering workflow, tooling, parts availability, operational tempo, and mission priority typical of Gulf of America offshore support operations. - Communicate directly with pilots and operational crews at each aircraft arrival and departure to ensure accurate exchange of status, discrepancies, and readiness information required for safe Part 135 operations. - Maintain the field base environment in compliance with Bristow’s safety, cleanliness, and housekeeping standards, ensuring a safe and mission‑ready work area for Gulf operations. - Assume full responsibility for each assigned aircraft, ensuring all maintenance actions are completed, documented, and returned to service per Bristow policy and FAA requirements. - Manage parts, materials, and inventory including submission of material requisitions, receipt of incoming parts, shipment of components to other GOA locations, and verification that all items meet Bristow inspection and traceability standards. - Maintain Ground Support Equipment (GSE) used at Gulf bases to Bristow standards, ensuring reliable support for daily offshore operations. - Complete and maintain all required aircraft records and maintenance documentation, including aircraft logs, company forms, technical entries, and documents required by 14 CFR Part 135, Bristow’s General Operations Manual, and company policy. - Inspect and verify all incoming parts and consumables immediately upon receipt, including removal of expired or shelf‑life items from inventory to maintain FAA compliance. - Request Secondary Maintenance Checks following maintenance, preventative maintenance, inspections, repairs, or alterations to ensure an additional layer of safety consistent with Bristow’s culture and RS manual guidance. - Maintain all calibrated tools, both company‑owned and personally owned, in accordance with Bristow’s calibrated tool tracking requirements for the assigned GOA base. - Perform other duties as assigned, supporting Bristow’s commitment to safe, reliable Part 135 rotor‑wing operations in the Gulf of America.

item.title: Apprentice - Paint Tech
Pilot - 14x14 - Gulf of America
Aviation Maintenance Technician - 145 Repair Station - 4x3 - Lake Charles, LA
Pilot (S76 PIC) - 14x14 - Houma, LA
BSEE Pilot (PIC) - 14x14 Schedule
Avionics Technician - 14x14 Shift - Houma, LA
Aviation Maintenance Technician - 28x28 - Suriname
item.description:
Applications are now being accepted for 135 Aviation Maintenance Technicians to provide support to the offshore oil & gas industry. 28x28 Rotational Shift Travel Covered Housing Provided Per Diem Other contract related benefits
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: - Accomplishing all work assigned to him/her in accordance with policies, procedures and specifications as set forth in the Company's and the manufacturers’ manuals. - Ensuring all aircraft are maintained to the highest airworthy and cosmetic standards and always reflect company standards. - Signing, recording and assuming responsibility for all work he/she performs. - Maintaining an attitude of alertness concerning possible unsafe or hazardous practices by him/her or other personnel which might be injurious to personnel or property. - Reporting all hazardous practices and equipment to his/her Lead Aviation Maintenance Technician. - Performing proficient and timely receiving inspections on incoming direct ship parts, stock transfer receipts and core parts handling when at a base without supply personnel. - Using all Company tools, parts, equipment, and materials properly and efficiently. - Communicating daily with the assigned flight crews with respect to the aircraft condition and ongoing maintenance to ensure the highest level of teamwork and visibility is achieved. - Maintaining a clean, safe and orderly work area to include personal and shop equipment. - Maintaining a clean work area during and after all job functions; - Providing on-the-job instruction and supervision of personnel assigned to assist him/her. - Staying current on manual changes, policy changes and rules concerning general conduct; and - Performing other duties as assigned.
MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: - Valid FAA A&P license required. - Minimum of two years’ rotorcraft maintenance experience required. - Must possess AW139 qualifications/training. - Must be able to operate MS Office Suite and Some Web-Based Computer Programs, (Excel, Word, and Outlook); - Must be available to work overtime as required; - Must be able to travel internationally as required; - Must possess or be able to possess a valid Passport; - Previous training or experience with company specific airframes preferred; - Previous Avionics or Sheet metal experience a plus; - Must be detail orientate, fast learner, and be a self-starter.

item.title: Mechanic I – Brazil
item.description:
We are looking for a Helicopter Mechanic I to perform preventive and corrective maintenance under supervision, ensuring the safety and airworthiness of our fleet, in compliance with ANAC regulations, manufacturers’ manuals, and Bristow’s core values.
Key responsibilities: - Carry out inspections, repairs, and scheduled/unscheduled maintenance under supervision. - Perform pre-flight, between-flight, and post-flight inspections according to technical manuals. - Ensure compliance with the Aircraft Maintenance Program and properly register activities in RAMCO. - Keep the workplace clean, organized, and safe.
Requirements: - At least 1 ANAC-issued CHT license. - Minimum of 18 months of proven aircraft maintenance experience. - Basic English (reading technical manuals). - Familiarization course on company-operated helicopter/engine type.
Nice to have: - Knowledge of aeronautical metrology and HUMS data analysis. - Experience with RAMCO system. - Alignment with Bristow’s values: Safety, Integrity, Teamwork, Passion, and Progress.

item.title: Avionics Technician- 4x3 - Lake Charles, LA
Aviation Maintenance Technician - 145 Repair Station - 4x3 - Lake Charles, LA
Avionics Technician- 14x14
Aviation Maintenance Technician - 145 Repair Station- 4x3 - Lake Charles, LA
Ground Operations Officer
item.description:
The main purpose of the Ground Operations Officer is to help and assist the pilots and act as the link between NHV Denmark and the client. Main Responsibilities - Make all the preparation for flights - Responsible for planning and coordination for all NHV Denmark’s flight operations - Understand the client´s business and needs, under the decision and supervision of the Ground Operations Manager - Liaise with customers as required/directed on delays and significant events. Assist with customer enquires or queries as required. - Liaise with line internal departments and customers as required on current operational matters. - Pilots scheduling - Ensure all administration is carried out to the required standard and regulations - Anticipate and co-ordinate required changes to the flight schedule and liaise with relevant departments. - Assist the colleagues and other ad hoc work from Operation or Ground Operations Manager - Responsible for complying with Information Security Management System (ISMS) policies, procedures, and controls and supporting information security objectives
Skills and Experience - Service minded - Language: Good knowledge of Danish and English is mandatory - Competent in the use of IT is mandatory - Good at communications - Ability to work under pressure - Experience in aviation environment is preferred - Experience in operations or coordination is preferred - Is a team player and helps out colleagues where needed

item.title: Finance Manager
item.description:
We are looking for a strategic and hands-on Finance Manager to lead our Group Finance function and strengthen our financial foundations for sustainable growth. Reporting directly to the CFO, the Finance Manager provides strategic and operational leadership across Finance, ensuring strong governance, regulatory compliance, commercial insight, and sustainable performance across the NHV Group. As accountable lead for Group accounting and tax compliance, you will partner closely with the CFO and senior leadership to shape international strategy, manage financial risk, and drive organizational performance. You combine strategic vision with operational excellence, continuously enhancing finance processes, systems, and policies to improve efficiency, transparency, and control.
Responsibilities and areas: - Finance Leadership & Team Management - Lead, coach, and develop a high-performing Finance team. - Build a future-ready finance function aligned with business needs. - Foster collaboration, accountability, and continuous professional development. - Travel to other bases as and when required. - Strategy, Governance & Policy Development - Develop and execute the Group finance vision and strategy. - Define and standardize Group accounting, tax, and transfer pricing policies. - Ensure strong governance, internal controls, and regulatory compliance across all entities. - Contribute to strategic planning and executive decision-making. - Partner with operational teams to drive performance and growth. - Financial Reporting & Consolidation - Oversee accurate and timely statutory and management reporting. - Lead consolidation across multiple legal entities. - Ensure integrity, transparency, and compliance in all reporting processes. - Act as primary contact for audits and external reviews. - Deliver high-quality financial analysis, insights, scenario planning, and advice on complex accounting and risk matters. - Process Improvement & Systems Optimization - Drive finance transformation and efficiency initiatives. - Optimise ERP systems and reporting tools. - Establish scalable processes for a dynamic international environment. - Ensure compliance with ISMS policies and safeguard company information and assets. - International Expansion & Compliance - Lead finance setup for new legal entities in collaboration with external advisors. - Ensure compliance with international tax frameworks and transfer pricing regulations. - Budget & Operational Management - Manage departmental budget and resource allocation. - Deliver finance outputs on time and to high quality standards. - Travel to operational bases as required.

item.title: AW139 Co-Pilot
item.description:
Are you an experienced AW139 Co-Pilot ready to operate in a professional offshore environment? At our Den Helder base, you will perform scheduled shuttle flights supporting offshore Oil & Gas operations, ensuring the highest standards of safety, efficiency and customer service. You will operate in a highly structured and safety-driven environment where professionalism and teamwork are essential.
Your Role As an AW139 Co-Pilot, you are responsible for executing flights in the safest and most cost-effective manner, in full compliance with aviation regulations, company procedures and operational standards.
What You’ll Be Doing - Flight Preparation & Execution - Execute flights in accordance with SOPs, limitations and performance requirements - Assisting the pilot-in-command with administrative flight duties - Understanding the client’s business & needs, under the decision and supervision of the Chief Pilot
Compliance & Professional Standards - Maintain full familiarity with national and international aviation legislation, agreed aviation practices and local procedures - Operate in accordance with the Operations Manual, FOM, FOIs and company procedures - Respect company policies including Code of Conduct, Safety, Quality and Drugs & Alcohol policies - Support the pilot-in-command in maintaining a proper standard of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance
Offshore & Team Responsibilities - Act responsible to the pilot-in-command in assisting to conduct an efficient flight - Support offshore Oil & Gas operations as assigned by the Chief Pilot - Remain flexible for operational needs - Lead by example and promote team spirit - Adapt to local culture, environment and client needs

item.title: Technical Director
item.description:
We are seeking an experienced and strategic Technical Director to lead and oversee all technical helicopter operations across our multi-base organization. Reporting directly to the CEO and serving on the Extended Executive Committee, you will hold full accountability for Maintenance (Part-145), CAMO, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, and Part-147 training. You will ensure safe, compliant, efficient, and customer-focused technical operations while driving performance, cost control, and fleet availability. This is a high-impact executive role combining operational leadership with strong business acumen and full P&L responsibility. You will lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, and sustainability programs, positioning the organization for future growth.
Responsibilities and areas Operational & Strategic Leadership - Lead and optimize all technical functions (Part-145 Maintenance, CAMO, Engineering, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, Part-147) with a strong business-driven and performance-focused approach. - Develop and execute technical strategies, structures, and processes aligned with corporate objectives, ensuring efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and operational excellence. - Drive organizational optimization and continuous improvement initiatives across maintenance and supply chain operations. - Ensure technical capabilities fully support operational delivery, customer commitments, and strategic growth objectives. - Establish, monitor, and report on KPIs covering safety, compliance, service quality, cost efficiency, and fleet availability. - Ensure compliance with ISMS policies and execute additional executive-level technical assignments as required.
Financial & Performance Management - Hold full P&L responsibility for technical operations, including maintenance budgets, supply chain expenditures, and lifecycle cost management. - Drive sustainable cost optimization and total cost of ownership improvements across the fleet. - Partner with Finance to ensure disciplined budgeting, forecasting, financial oversight, and structured performance reporting. - Balance profitability objectives with uncompromising safety and regulatory compliance standards.
People Leadership & Culture - Provide strategic leadership to multi-location technical teams, building strong leadership capability, succession planning, and skills development. - Establish clear performance goals, accountability frameworks, and KPI-driven management structures. - Foster a culture aligned with company values, embedding safety, accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. - Promote strong cross-functional alignment and transparent executive communication.
Safety, Compliance & Governance - Ensure full compliance with AOC, (B)CAA, EASA/CAA/ICAO regulations, and internal standards across all technical domains. - Oversee governance of Accountable Managers and Postholders (Part-145, CAMO, Supply Chain, Fleet Management, Part-147). - Maintain robust operational controls, reporting systems, documentation, approvals, and regulatory records. - Champion airworthiness, safety, quality assurance, and consistent adherence to procedures and best practices.
Transformation, Innovation & Continuous Improvement - Lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, sustainability programs, and SAF adoption. - Introduce innovative technologies and structured change management to enhance reliability, safety, availability, and cost efficiency. - Drive continuous performance improvement across all technical and logistical functions.
Stakeholder Management - Build and maintain strong relationships with OEMs, suppliers, MROs, regulators, aviation authorities, and customers. - Represent the technical organization at executive and regulatory levels. - Ensure external partnerships and contractual commitments align with internal technical strategy and support business growth.

item.title: Flight Operations Officer
item.description:
The Flight Operations Officer is responsible for the execution of the daily base flight schedule for our base in Den Helder. They will interface with a number of internal and external stakeholders to manage the safe, compliant and efficient delivery of the program, whilst upholding a high standard of service to NHV’s clients.
Responsibilities and areas: - Flight Planning & Coordination - Prepare and maintain daily flight schedules, including flight watch and monitoring. - Anticipate and coordinate required changes to the flight schedule. - Provide operational paperwork and briefings to flight crews. - Monitor weather and NOTAMs relevant to flight operations.
- Crew Management - Manage pilot scheduling and ensure compliance with Flight Time Limitations (FTL). - Liaise with crew members regarding operational updates and changes.
- Client & Stakeholder Liaison - Act as a key point of contact for clients regarding flight changes, disruptions, and queries. - Liaise with Air Traffic Control and airport authorities as needed. - Communicate with internal departments (Engineering, Ground Operations, etc.) to ensure fleet availability and operational readiness.
- Administration & Compliance - Ensure all operational and customer reports are completed accurately and on time. - Maintain high standards of documentation in line with regulatory and company requirements.

item.title: AW139 Captain
item.description:
Are you an experienced AW139 Captain ready to operate in a professional offshore environment? At our Den Helder base, you will perform scheduled shuttle flights supporting offshore Oil & Gas operations, ensuring the highest standards of safety, efficiency and customer service. You will operate in a highly structured and safety-driven environment where professionalism and teamwork are essential.
Your Role As an AW139 Captain, you are responsible for executing flights in the safest and most cost-effective manner, in full compliance with aviation regulations, company procedures and operational standards. You report to the Chief Pilot and play a key role in representing the company towards our offshore clients.
What You’ll Be Doing - Flight Preparation & Execution - Perform full technical preparation of flights (route planning, fuel calculations, aircraft inspection) - Execute flights in accordance with SOPs, limitations and performance requirements - Ensure all aircraft safety systems are fully operational - Complete accurate pre- and post-flight administration - Report any incidents, defects or irregularities - Compliance & Professional Standards - Maintain full familiarity with national and international aviation legislation - Operate in accordance with the Operations Manual, FOM, FOIs and company procedures - Respect company policies including Safety, Quality and Drugs & Alcohol policies - Maintain high standards of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance - Offshore & Team Responsibilities - Support offshore Oil & Gas operations as assigned by the Chief Pilot - Remain flexible for operational needs - Demonstrate strong customer service awareness - Lead by example and promote team spirit - Adapt to local culture, environment and client needs

item.title: Experienced HHO Operator – Licensed Engineer
item.description:
Are you an experienced Helicopter Hoist Operator and licensed engineer with a passion for safety and efficiency? Join our team to execute both scheduled and unscheduled Helicopter Hoist Operations (HHO) flights and crew change flights.
The helicopter Hoist Operator plays a critical role combining technical skills as a Licensed Line Engineer working on the helicopter and performing hoist flights hoisting passengers to the wind farms. Safety management and good communication play a crucial role. You work in a small team of engineers, pilots and hoist operators following the helicopter to different operational locations. You report functionally to the Maintenance Lead who oversees the maintenance and hierarchically to the Base Manager in Esbjerg. You will follow the aircraft in bases in Denmark, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Belgium or any other European country the customer requests.
Responsibilities and Main Results Areas - Helicopter Hoist operator (HHO) - Carry out HHO flight duties, in accordance with NHV Operations SOP's. - Know the NHV procedures and the Flight Operations Manual. - Inspect hoist systems, cables, hooks, harnesses, and safety equipment. - Conduct safety briefings with passengers and flight crew. - Coordinate passenger- and aircraft handling procedures before and after flights. - Assist with the technical preparation for the flight(s). - Operate hoist controls smoothly to raise/lower personnel or cargo. - Provide precise directional guidance to the pilot (clearance, obstacles, positioning). - Immediately halt operations if unsafe conditions arise. - Use standardized aviation terminology. - Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues. - Respecting the code of conduct and overall behaviour towards the whole NHV team, as well as respecting the Drugs & Alcohol policy. - Line Maintenance - Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the BCAA/EASA and MOE instructions. - Work on aircraft and aircraft components as per Work Order, whilst respecting any restriction applicable. - Ensure all safety regulations are followed. - Report any safety hazard and mandatory reportable occurrence via the NHV occurrence reporting system. - Ensure correct following and performing of all maintenance instructions as detailed in the approved maintenance data and ensure correct following of all MOE procedures. - Report if any procedure is inaccurate, incomplete or ambiguous. - Record all performed maintenance in the work file and ensure all tasks are properly certified. - Raise non-routine tasks or defect entries in the system for all unscheduled findings. - Ensure Independent Inspections are performed on all safety critical tasks. - Keep the workspace clean and tidy and keep the tools and equipment in good working order. - Ensure, together with all engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business continuity. - Ensure maintenance is completed before due and within the appropriate time limits to protect the operational needs. - Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain and support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment. Ensure, based on pilot & other relevant information, daily problem analysis & problem solving.

item.title: B1.3 Engineer AW139
item.description:
Are you an experienced EASA Part 66 B1.3 Licensed Engineer ready to work in a dynamic offshore helicopter environment? At our Den Helder base, you will play a key role in keeping our AW139 fleet mission-ready and safe. You’ll join a close-knit team of 8 engineers, working in a professional yet supportive environment where safety, teamwork and technical excellence come first.
Your Role As a B1.3 Engineer, you are responsible for performing and certifying line maintenance in accordance with approved maintenance data and company procedures. You work closely with fellow engineers under the supervision of the Shift Supervisor, reporting hierarchically to the Base Manager and functionally to the Maintenance Lead. You are more than a certifying engineer — you are a problem solver, safety ambassador and key contributor to operational continuity.
What You’ll Be Doing - Line Maintenance & Technical Excellence - Perform and certify maintenance tasks in accordance with approved maintenance data - Execute work orders while respecting all applicable restrictions - Raise non-routine tasks and defect entries for unscheduled findings - Ensure Independent Inspections on safety-critical tasks - Record and certify all maintenance accurately in the work file - Safety & Compliance - Follow all safety regulations and MOE procedures - Report safety hazards and occurrences via the reporting system - Flag inaccurate, incomplete or unclear procedures - Maintain a clean and orderly workspace with properly maintained tools - Operational Support - Contribute to flexible and efficient work planning - Ensure maintenance is completed within operational deadlines - Coordinate with Supply Chain, Stores and support services to guarantee parts availability - Perform daily technical analysis and problem-solving based on crew feedback
What You Bring Experience - Minimum 5 years of experience as a licensed engineer - At least 2 years hands-on experience on AW139 - Experience in multiple aviation organizations is a plus Qualifications - EASA Part 66 B1.3 License - Type Rating AW139 (required) - H175 or H160 Type Rating (advantageous) Skills - Strong troubleshooting and logical thinking skills - Confident communicator (flight crew debriefings & shift handovers) - Able to work independently in less controlled environments - Proactive, flexible and solution-oriented - ICT literate (Rusada Envision knowledge is a plus) - Professional level of English - Living within commuting distance of Den Helder
What We Offer Join the Thrilling World of Aviation. Become part of an international helicopter services company that combines a youthful mindset with over 25 years of industry expertise. We operate in a highly professional offshore environment where teamwork, safety and technical precision are paramount. Permanent (indefinite) contract, Competitive salary package, 14 days ON / 14 days OFF roster, 183 duties per year, Early/Late shift pattern, A supportive team of 8 engineers led by an experienced Shift Supervisor/Maintenance Lead.

item.title: B1.3 and B2 H175 Engineer
item.description:
Are you an experienced licensed engineer on H175 with a passion for safety and efficiency? Join our team to execute both scheduled and unscheduled crew change flights and Helicopter Hoist Operations (HHO) flights.
The helicopter B1.3 or B2 Engineer plays a critical role ensuring the helicopter is ready and fit for flight and is in an airworthy condition. Performing all required scheduled and unscheduled line maintenance. Safety management and good communication play a crucial role. You work in a small team of engineers, pilots and hoist operators following the helicopter to different operational locations for crew changes and hoisting on wind farms. You report functionally to the Maintenance Lead who oversees the maintenance and hierarchically to the Base Manager in Esbjerg. You will follow the aircraft in bases in Denmark, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Belgium or any other European country the customer requests. Responsibilities and Main Results Areas Line Maintenance · Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the BCAA/EASA and MOE instructions · Work on aircraft and aircraft components as per Work Order, whilst respecting any restriction applicable. · Ensure all safety regulations are followed · Report any safety hazard and mandatory reportable occurrence via the NHV occurrence reporting system. · Ensure correct following and performing of all maintenance instructions as detailed in the approved maintenance data and ensure correct following of all MOE procedures. · Report if any procedure is inaccurate, incomplete or ambiguous. · Record all performed maintenance in the work file and ensure all tasks are properly certified. · Raise non-routine tasks or defect entries in the system for all unscheduled findings. · Ensure Independent Inspections are performed on all safety critical tasks. · Keep the workspace clean and tidy and keep the tools and equipment in good working order. · Ensure, together with all engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business continuity. · Ensure maintenance is completed before due and within the appropriate time limits to protect the operational needs. · Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain and support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment. · Ensure, based on pilot & other relevant information, daily problem analysis & problem solving. Hoist Operations · A combination of an Engineering position and a Helicopter Hoist Operator (HHO) position is a possibility but not a requirement.

item.title: H175 Co-Pilot
item.description: To execute scheduled offshore energy flights in the safest and most efficient manner in accordance with aviation regulation and whilst upholding NHV standards. Responsibilities - Assisting the Pilot-in-Command with the technical preparation of flights including, but not limited to, route planning, fuel calculation, performance limitations and general inspection of the helicopter - Ensuring that all pre and post flight administration is completed accurately under the supervision of the Pilot-in-Command - Assisting the Pilot-in-Command as required with the raising of any reportable incidents and issues post flight via the correct channel. - Conducting pilot duties under the supervision of the Pilot-in-Command whilst fully complying with all safety rules, requirements, limitations and SOPs with full regard to the helicopter type and scope of flight operation - Working alongside the Pilot-in-Command to ensure that all safety systems and equipment are functioning correctly - Maintaining a good working knowledge of NHV Ops Manuals, Notices To Pilots, other related manuals, instructions and procedures - Consistently demonstrating a good awareness of customer service requirements and understanding the client's business & needs, under the supervision of the Chief Pilot. - Performing other assigned flying duties as required - Maintaining familiarity with all relevant national and international air legislation, agreed aviation practices and local procedures. - Maintaining the correct standard of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance. - Completing tasks allocated by the Chief Pilot as required - Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues. - Respecting the code of conduct and overall behaviour towards the whole NHV team, as well as adhering to the Drugs & Alcohol policy.

item.title: Mechanic - Aberdeen
B2 Engineer
Technical Safety and Compliance Manager
item.description:
The DK Technical Safety and Compliance Manager is the nominated person for the DK AOC and for all QHSE matters. Reporting to the Accountable Manager, you will shape and maintain the organisations commitment to exceptional quality standards.
The role will involve travel to NHV bases and supplier sites will be required. The Technical Safety and Compliance Manager co-operates closely with all organisational subdivisions of NHV. Responsibilities and areas · Develop and execute an internal audit plan and lead and manage internal audits, including scoping, planning. · Collaborate with cross functional teams to ensure timely completion of audit projects and prepare and present audit findings and recommendations. · Develop and improve audit checklists and audit reports. · Execute product and process audits to check the conformity of the work steps according to the technical product and process requirements, to verify compliance of operational processes with aviation regulations, industry standards, corporate guidelines, or operational procedures, either internally or externally. · Coach and train auditee to understand the requirements. · Documentation of audit results in audit reports, definition of deviations, and discussion of the results with the relevant departments or suppliers. · Documentation of deviations, monitoring deadlines, and tracking corrective and preventive actions. · Support of the departments in the definition, initiation, and implementation of corrective and preventive actions. · Daily management of safety cases in the reporting system
Skills & experience · Subject Matter Expert in Part 145 and Part M, OR extensive experience within Health, Safety & Environment disciplines · Experience in a similar role with aviation regulations knowledge · Experience in risk analysis · Languages: Danish and English (professional level) · Ability to follow company procedures and processes (operational, quality, safety …) · Knowledge of continuous improvement environment · Knowledge/understanding of regulatory environments - Highly desirable: - P145 - Continuing airworthiness
What we offer
You'll be welcomed into an exciting company. Based on your skills and competences, you'll be offered a competitive salary with benefits. The opportunity can be taken to widen your knowledge and skills.

item.title: Helicopter mechanic - Lanvéoc
B2 Engineer - Lanvéoc
